title: Support function calls with random arguments in constraints
body:
## Summary

Function calls that take random variable arguments in constraint expressions (IEEE 1800-2017 Section 18.5.12) are evaluated with pre-randomization default values instead of solver-determined values, producing incorrect randomization results.

## Current Behavior

- Constraint `value >= get_min_value(mask)` where `get_min_value(bit[7:0] m)` returns `m & 8'hF0` and `mask` is a `rand` variable: the function is evaluated at constraint setup time with `mask = 0` (default), producing `get_min_value(0) = 0`
- The solver sees a constant `0` instead of the symbolic expression `(bvand mask #xf0)`, so the constraint is effectively `value >= 0` — always trivially satisfied
- Randomized values violate the intended constraint relationship

## Expected Behavior

- Function calls with random variable arguments should be inlined into SMT constraints so the solver evaluates them with the solved values
- `value >= get_min_value(mask)` should produce SMT constraint `(bvuge value (bvand mask #xf0))`
- Randomized values should satisfy `value >= (mask & 0xF0)` for every randomization

## Source

- planvtech/PlanV_Verilator_Feature_Tests#27

---
PR is coming.
